net.ISA_28_22: So now, do not mock, or your chains will become heavier! For I have heard a message about decreed destruction, from the Sovereign Lord of Heaven’s Armies against the entire land.
net.ISA_28_23: Pay attention and listen to my message. Be attentive and listen to what I have to say!
net.ISA_28_24: Does a farmer just keep on plowing at planting time? Does he keep breaking up and harrowing his ground?
net.ISA_28_25: Once he has leveled its surface, does he not scatter the seed of the caraway plant, sow the seed of the cumin plant, and plant the wheat, barley, and grain in their designated places?
net.ISA_28_26: His God instructs him; he teaches him the principles of agriculture.
net.ISA_28_27: Certainly caraway seed is not threshed with a sledge, nor is the wheel of a cart rolled over cumin seed. Certainly caraway seed is beaten with a stick, and cumin seed with a flail.
net.ISA_28_28: Grain is crushed, though one certainly does not thresh it forever. The wheel of one’s wagon rolls over it, but his horses do not crush it.
net.ISA_28_29: This also comes from the Lord of Heaven’s Armies, who gives supernatural guidance and imparts great wisdom.
net.ISA_29_1: Ariel is as good as dead — Ariel, the town David besieged! Keep observing your annual rituals; celebrate your festivals on schedule.
net.ISA_29_2: I will threaten Ariel, and she will mourn intensely and become like an altar hearth before me.
net.ISA_29_3: I will lay siege to you on all sides; I will besiege you with troops; I will raise siege works against you.
net.ISA_29_4: You will fall; while lying on the ground you will speak; from the dust where you lie, your words will be heard. Your voice will sound like a spirit speaking from the underworld; from the dust you will chirp as if muttering an incantation.
net.ISA_29_5: But the horde of invaders will be like fine dust, the horde of tyrants like chaff that is blown away. It will happen suddenly, in a flash.
net.ISA_29_6: Judgment will come from the Lord of Heaven’s Armies, accompanied by thunder, earthquake, and a loud noise, by a strong gale, a windstorm, and a consuming flame of fire.
net.ISA_29_7: It will be like a dream, a night vision. There will be a horde from all the nations that fight against Ariel, those who attack her and her stronghold and besiege her.
net.ISA_29_8: It will be like a hungry man dreaming that he is eating, only to awaken and find that his stomach is empty. It will be like a thirsty man dreaming that he is drinking, only to awaken and find that he is still weak and his thirst unquenched. So it will be for the horde from all the nations that fight against Mount Zion. God’s People Are Spiritually Insensitive
net.ISA_29_9: You will be shocked and amazed! You are totally blind! They are drunk, but not because of wine; they stagger, but not because of beer.
net.ISA_29_10: For the Lord has poured out on you a strong urge to sleep deeply. He has shut your eyes (you prophets), and covered your heads (you seers).
net.ISA_29_11: To you this entire prophetic revelation is like words in a sealed scroll. When they hand it to one who can read and say, “Read this,” he responds, “I can’t, because it is sealed.”
net.ISA_29_12: Or when they hand the scroll to one who can’t read and say, “Read this,” he says, “I can’t read.”
net.ISA_29_13: The Lord says, “These people say they are loyal to me; they say wonderful things about me, but they are not really loyal to me. Their worship consists of nothing but man-made ritual.
net.ISA_29_14: Therefore I will again do an amazing thing for these people— an absolutely extraordinary deed. Wise men will have nothing to say, the sages will have no explanations.”
net.ISA_29_15: Those who try to hide their plans from the Lord are as good as dead, who do their work in secret and boast, “Who sees us? Who knows what we’re doing?”
net.ISA_29_16: Your thinking is perverse! Should the potter be regarded as clay? Should the thing made say about its maker, “He didn’t make me”? Or should the pottery say about the potter, “He doesn’t understand”? Changes Are Coming
net.ISA_29_17: In just a very short time Lebanon will turn into an orchard, and the orchard will be considered a forest.
net.ISA_29_18: At that time the deaf will be able to hear words read from a scroll, and the eyes of the blind will be able to see through deep darkness.
net.ISA_29_19: The downtrodden will again rejoice in the Lord; the poor among humankind will take delight in the Holy One of Israel.
net.ISA_29_20: For tyrants will disappear, those who taunt will vanish, and all those who love to do wrong will be eliminated —
net.ISA_29_21: those who bear false testimony against a person, who entrap the one who arbitrates at the city gate and deprive the innocent of justice by making false charges.
net.ISA_29_22: So this is what the Lord, the one who delivered Abraham, has said to the family of Jacob: “Jacob will no longer be ashamed; their faces will no longer show their embarrassment.
net.ISA_29_23: For when they see their children, whom I will produce among them, they will honor my name. They will honor the Holy One of Jacob; they will respect the God of Israel.
net.ISA_29_24: Those who stray morally will gain understanding; those who complain will acquire insight.
net.ISA_30_1: “The rebellious children are as good as dead,” says the Lord, “those who make plans without consulting me, who form alliances without consulting my Spirit, and thereby compound their sin.
net.ISA_30_2: They travel down to Egypt without seeking my will, seeking Pharaoh’s protection, and looking for safety in Egypt’s protective shade.
net.ISA_30_3: But Pharaoh’s protection will bring you nothing but shame, and the safety of Egypt’s protective shade nothing but humiliation.
net.ISA_30_4: Though his officials are in Zoan and his messengers arrive at Hanes,
net.ISA_30_5: all will be put to shame because of a nation that cannot help them, who cannot give them aid or help, but only shame and disgrace.”
net.ISA_30_6: This is an oracle about the animals in the Negev: Through a land of distress and danger, inhabited by lionesses and roaring lions, by snakes and darting adders, they transport their wealth on the backs of donkeys, their riches on the humps of camels, to a nation that cannot help them.
net.ISA_30_7: Egypt is totally incapable of helping. For this reason I call her “Proud one who is silenced.”
net.ISA_30_8: Now go, write it down on a tablet in their presence, inscribe it on a scroll, so that it might be preserved for a future time as an enduring witness.
net.ISA_30_9: For these are rebellious people— they are lying children, children unwilling to obey the Lord’s law.
net.ISA_30_10: They say to the visionaries, “See no more visions!” and to the seers, “Don’t relate messages to us about what is right! Tell us nice things; relate deceptive messages.
net.ISA_30_11: Turn aside from the way; stray off the path. Remove from our presence the Holy One of Israel.”
net.ISA_30_12: For this reason this is what the Holy One of Israel says: “You have rejected this message; you trust instead in your ability to oppress and trick, and rely on that kind of behavior.
net.ISA_30_13: So this sin will become your downfall. You will be like a high wall that bulges and cracks and is ready to collapse; it crumbles suddenly, in a flash.
net.ISA_30_14: It shatters in pieces like a clay jar, so shattered to bits that none of it can be salvaged. Among its fragments one cannot find a shard large enough to scoop a hot coal from a fire or to skim off water from a cistern.”
net.ISA_30_15: For this is what the Sovereign Lord, the Holy One of Israel says: “If you repented and patiently waited for me, you would be delivered; if you calmly trusted in me, you would find strength, but you are unwilling.
net.ISA_30_16: You say, ‘No, we will flee on horses,’ so you will indeed flee. You say, ‘We will ride on fast horses,’ so your pursuers will be fast.
net.ISA_30_17: One thousand will scurry at the battle cry of one enemy soldier; at the battle cry of five enemy soldiers you will all run away, until the remaining few are as isolated as a flagpole on a mountaintop or a signal flag on a hill.” The Lord Will Not Abandon His People
net.ISA_30_18: For this reason the Lord is ready to show you mercy; he sits on his throne, ready to have compassion on you. Indeed, the Lord is a just God; all who wait for him in faith will be blessed.
net.ISA_30_19: For people will live in Zion; in Jerusalem you will weep no more. When he hears your cry of despair, he will indeed show you mercy; when he hears it, he will respond to you.
net.ISA_30_20: The Lord will give you distress to eat and suffering to drink; but your teachers will no longer be hidden; your eyes will see them.
net.ISA_30_21: You will hear a word spoken behind you, saying, “This is the correct way, walk in it,” whether you are heading to the right or the left.
net.ISA_30_22: You will desecrate your silver-plated idols and your gold-plated images. You will throw them away as if they were a menstrual rag, saying to them, “Get out!”
net.ISA_30_23: He will water the seed you plant in the ground, and the ground will produce crops in abundance. At that time your cattle will graze in wide pastures.
net.ISA_30_24: The oxen and donkeys used in plowing will eat seasoned feed winnowed with a shovel and pitchfork.
net.ISA_30_25: On every high mountain and every high hill there will be streams flowing with water, at the time of great slaughter when the fortified towers collapse.
net.ISA_30_26: The light of the full moon will be like the sun’s glare, and the sun’s glare will be seven times brighter, like the light of seven days, when the Lord binds up his people’s fractured bones and heals their severe wound.
net.ISA_30_27: Look, the name of the Lord comes from a distant place in raging anger and awesome splendor. He speaks angrily, and his word is like destructive fire.
net.ISA_30_28: His battle cry overwhelms like a flooding river that reaches one’s neck. He shakes the nations in a sieve that isolates the chaff; he puts a bit into the mouth of the nations and leads them to destruction.
net.ISA_30_29: You will sing as you do in the evening when you are celebrating a festival. You will be happy like one who plays a flute as he goes to the mountain of the Lord, the Rock who shelters Israel.
net.ISA_30_30: The Lord will give a mighty shout and intervene in power, with furious anger and flaming, destructive fire, with a driving rainstorm and hailstones.
net.ISA_30_31: Indeed, the Lord’s shout will shatter Assyria; he will beat them with a club.
net.ISA_30_32: Every blow from his punishing cudgel with which the Lord will beat them will be accompanied by music from the tambourine and harp, and he will attack them with his weapons.
net.ISA_30_33: For the burial place is already prepared; it has been made deep and wide for the king. The firewood is piled high on it. The Lord’s breath, like a stream flowing with brimstone, will ignite it.
net.ISA_31_1: Those who go down to Egypt for help are as good as dead; those who rely on war horses, and trust in Egypt’s many chariots and in their many, many horsemen. But they do not rely on the Holy One of Israel and do not seek help from the Lord.
net.ISA_31_2: Yet he too is wise and he will bring disaster; he does not retract his decree. He will attack the wicked nation, and the nation that helps those who commit sin.
net.ISA_31_3: The Egyptians are mere humans, not God; their horses are made of flesh, not spirit. The Lord will strike with his hand; the one who helps will stumble and the one being helped will fall. Together they will perish. The Lord Will Defend Zion
net.ISA_31_4: Indeed, this is what the Lord has said to me: “The Lord will be like a growling lion, like a young lion growling over its prey. Though a whole group of shepherds gathers against it, it is not afraid of their shouts or intimidated by their yelling. In this same way the Lord of Heaven’s Armies will descend to do battle on Mount Zion and on its hill.
net.ISA_31_5: Just as birds hover over a nest, so the Lord of Heaven’s Armies will protect Jerusalem. He will protect and deliver it; as he passes over he will rescue it.”
net.ISA_31_6: You Israelites! Return to the one you have so blatantly rebelled against!
net.ISA_31_7: For at that time every one will get rid of the silver and gold idols your hands sinfully made.
net.ISA_31_8: “Assyria will fall by a sword, but not one human-made; a sword not made by humankind will destroy them. They will run away from this sword and their young men will be forced to do hard labor.
net.ISA_31_9: They will surrender their stronghold because of fear; their officers will be afraid of the Lord’s battle flag.” This is what the Lord says— the one whose fire is in Zion, whose firepot is in Jerusalem.
net.ISA_32_1: Look, a king will promote fairness; officials will promote justice.
net.ISA_32_2: Each of them will be like a shelter from the wind and a refuge from a rainstorm; like streams of water in a dry region and like the shade of a large cliff in a parched land.
net.ISA_32_3: Eyes will no longer be blind and ears will be attentive.
net.ISA_32_4: The mind that acts rashly will possess discernment, and the tongue that stutters will speak with ease and clarity.
net.ISA_32_5: A fool will no longer be called honorable; a deceiver will no longer be called principled.
net.ISA_32_6: For a fool speaks disgraceful things; his mind plans out sinful deeds. He commits godless deeds and says misleading things about the Lord; he gives the hungry nothing to satisfy their appetite and gives the thirsty nothing to drink.
net.ISA_32_7: A deceiver’s methods are evil; he dreams up evil plans to ruin the poor with lies, even when the needy are in the right.
net.ISA_32_8: An honorable man makes honorable plans; his honorable character gives him security. The Lord Will Give True Security
net.ISA_32_9: You complacent women, get up and listen to me! You carefree daughters, pay attention to what I say!
net.ISA_32_10: In a year’s time you carefree ones will shake with fear, for the grape harvest will fail, and the fruit harvest will not arrive.
net.ISA_32_11: Tremble, you complacent ones! Shake with fear, you carefree ones! Strip off your clothes and expose yourselves— put sackcloth around your waists.
net.ISA_32_12: Mourn over the field, over the delightful fields and the fruitful vine.
net.ISA_32_13: Mourn over the land of my people, which is overgrown with thorns and briers, and over all the once-happy houses in the city filled with revelry.
net.ISA_32_14: For the fortress is neglected; the once-crowded city is abandoned. Hill and watchtower are permanently uninhabited. Wild donkeys love to go there, and flocks graze there.
net.ISA_32_15: This desolation will continue until new life is poured out on us from heaven. Then the wilderness will become an orchard and the orchard will be considered a forest.
net.ISA_32_16: Justice will settle down in the wilderness and fairness will live in the orchard.
net.ISA_32_17: Fairness will produce peace and result in lasting security.
net.ISA_32_18: My people will live in peaceful settlements, in secure homes, and in safe, quiet places.
net.ISA_32_19: Even if the forest is destroyed and the city is annihilated,
net.ISA_32_20: you will be blessed, you who plant seed by all the banks of the streams, you who let your ox and donkey graze.
net.ISA_33_1: The destroyer is as good as dead, you who have not been destroyed! The deceitful one is as good as dead, the one whom others have not deceived! When you are through destroying, you will be destroyed; when you finish deceiving, others will deceive you!
net.ISA_33_2: Lord, be merciful to us! We wait for you. Give us strength each morning. Deliver us when distress comes.
net.ISA_33_3: The nations run away when they hear a loud noise; the nations scatter when you spring into action!
net.ISA_33_4: Your plunder disappears as if locusts were eating it; they swarm over it like locusts.
net.ISA_33_5: The Lord is exalted, indeed, he lives in heaven; he fills Zion with justice and fairness.
net.ISA_33_6: He is your constant source of stability; he abundantly provides safety and great wisdom; he gives all this to those who fear him.
net.ISA_33_7: Look, ambassadors cry out in the streets; messengers sent to make peace weep bitterly.
net.ISA_33_8: Highways are empty, there are no travelers. Treaties are broken, witnesses are despised, human life is treated with disrespect.
net.ISA_33_9: The land dries up and withers away; the forest of Lebanon shrivels up and decays. Sharon is like the arid rift valley; Bashan and Carmel are parched.
net.ISA_33_10: “Now I will rise up,” says the Lord. “Now I will exalt myself; now I will magnify myself.
net.ISA_33_11: You conceive straw, you give birth to chaff; your breath is a fire that destroys you.
net.ISA_33_12: The nations will be burned to ashes; like thorn bushes that have been cut down, they will be set on fire.
net.ISA_33_13: You who are far away, listen to what I have done! You who are close by, recognize my strength.”
net.ISA_33_14: Sinners are afraid in Zion; panic grips the godless. They say, “Who among us can coexist with destructive fire? Who among us can coexist with unquenchable fire?”
net.ISA_33_15: The one who lives uprightly and speaks honestly; the one who refuses to profit from oppressive measures and rejects a bribe; the one who does not plot violent crimes and does not seek to harm others —
net.ISA_33_16: this is the person who will live in a secure place; he will find safety in the rocky, mountain strongholds; he will have food and a constant supply of water.
net.ISA_33_17: You will see a king in his splendor; you will see a wide land.
net.ISA_33_18: Your mind will recall the terror you experienced, and you will ask yourselves, “Where is the scribe? Where is the one who weighs the money? Where is the one who counts the towers?”
net.ISA_33_19: You will no longer see a defiant people whose language you do not comprehend, whose derisive speech you do not understand.
net.ISA_33_20: Look at Zion, the city where we hold religious festivals! You will see Jerusalem, a peaceful settlement, a tent that stays put; its stakes will never be pulled up; none of its ropes will snap in two.
net.ISA_33_21: Instead the Lord will rule there as our mighty king. Rivers and wide streams will flow through it; no war galley will enter; no large ships will sail through.
net.ISA_33_22: For the Lord, our ruler, the Lord, our commander, the Lord, our king— he will deliver us.
net.ISA_33_23: Though at this time your ropes are slack, the mast is not secured, and the sail is not unfurled, at that time you will divide up a great quantity of loot; even the lame will drag off plunder.
net.ISA_33_24: No resident of Zion will say, “I am ill”; the people who live there will have their sin forgiven.
net.ISA_34_1: Come near, you nations, and listen! Pay attention, you people! The earth and everything it contains must listen, the world and everything that lives in it.
net.ISA_34_2: For the Lord is angry at all the nations and furious with all their armies. He will annihilate them and slaughter them.
net.ISA_34_3: Their slain will be left unburied, their corpses will stink; the hills will soak up their blood.
net.ISA_34_4: All the stars in the sky will fade away, the sky will roll up like a scroll; all its stars will wither, like a leaf withers and falls from a vine or a fig withers and falls from a tree.
net.ISA_34_5: He says, “Indeed, my sword has slaughtered heavenly powers. Look, it now descends on Edom, on the people I will annihilate in judgment.”
net.ISA_34_6: The Lord’s sword is dripping with blood, it is covered with fat; it drips with the blood of young rams and goats and is covered with the fat of rams’ kidneys. For the Lord is holding a sacrifice in Bozrah, a bloody slaughter in the land of Edom.
net.ISA_34_7: Wild oxen will be slaughtered along with them, as well as strong bulls. Their land is drenched with blood, their soil is covered with fat.
net.ISA_34_8: For the Lord has planned a day of revenge, a time when he will repay Edom for her hostility toward Zion.
net.ISA_34_9: Edom’s streams will be turned into pitch and her soil into brimstone; her land will become burning pitch.
net.ISA_34_10: Night and day it will burn; its smoke will ascend continually. Generation after generation it will be a wasteland, and no one will ever pass through it again.
net.ISA_34_11: Owls and wild animals will live there, all kinds of wild birds will settle in it. The Lord will stretch out over her the measuring line of ruin and the plumb line of destruction.
net.ISA_34_12: Her nobles will have nothing left to call a kingdom, and all her officials will disappear.
net.ISA_34_13: Her fortresses will be overgrown with thorns; thickets and weeds will grow in her fortified cities. Jackals will settle there; ostriches will live there.
net.ISA_34_14: Wild animals and wild dogs will congregate there; wild goats will bleat to one another. Yes, nocturnal animals will rest there and make for themselves a nest.
net.ISA_34_15: Owls will make nests and lay eggs there; they will hatch them and protect them. Yes, hawks will gather there, each with its mate.
net.ISA_34_16: Carefully read the scroll of the Lord! Not one of these creatures will be missing, none will lack a mate. For the Lord has issued the decree, and his own spirit gathers them.
net.ISA_34_17: He assigns them their allotment; he measures out their assigned place. They will live there permanently; they will settle in it through successive generations.
net.ISA_35_1: Let the wilderness and desert be happy; let the arid rift valley rejoice and bloom like a lily!
net.ISA_35_2: Let it richly bloom; let it rejoice and shout with delight! It is given the grandeur of Lebanon, the splendor of Carmel and Sharon. They will see the grandeur of the Lord, the splendor of our God.
net.ISA_35_3: Strengthen the hands that have gone limp, steady the knees that shake.
net.ISA_35_4: Tell those who panic, “Be strong! Do not fear! Look, your God comes to avenge; with divine retribution he comes to deliver you.”
net.ISA_35_5: Then blind eyes will open, deaf ears will hear.
net.ISA_35_6: Then the lame will leap like a deer, the mute tongue will shout for joy; for water will burst forth in the wilderness, streams in the arid rift valley.
net.ISA_35_7: The dry soil will become a pool of water, the parched ground springs of water. Where jackals once lived and sprawled out, grass, reeds, and papyrus will grow.
net.ISA_35_8: A thoroughfare will be there— it will be called the Way of Holiness. The unclean will not travel on it; it is reserved for those authorized to use it — fools will not stray into it.
net.ISA_35_9: No lions will be there, no ferocious wild animals will be on it — they will not be found there. Those delivered from bondage will travel on it,
net.ISA_35_10: those whom the Lord has ransomed will return that way. They will enter Zion with a happy shout. Unending joy will crown them, happiness and joy will overwhelm them; grief and suffering will disappear.
net.ISA_36_1: In the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah’s reign, King Sennacherib of Assyria marched up against all the fortified cities of Judah and captured them.
net.ISA_36_2: The king of Assyria sent his chief adviser from Lachish to King Hezekiah in Jerusalem, along with a large army. The chief adviser stood at the conduit of the upper pool that is located on the road to the field where they wash and dry cloth.
net.ISA_36_3: Eliakim son of Hilkiah, the palace supervisor, accompanied by Shebna the scribe and Joah son of Asaph, the secretary, went out to meet him.
net.ISA_36_4: The chief adviser said to them, “Tell Hezekiah: ‘This is what the great king, the king of Assyria, says: “What is your source of confidence?
net.ISA_36_5: Your claim to have a strategy and military strength is just empty talk. In whom are you trusting, that you would dare to rebel against me?
net.ISA_36_6: Look, you must be trusting in Egypt, that splintered reed staff. If someone leans on it for support, it punctures his hand and wounds him. That is what Pharaoh king of Egypt does to all who trust in him!
net.ISA_36_7: Perhaps you will tell me, ‘We are trusting in the Lord our God.’ But Hezekiah is the one who eliminated his high places and altars and then told the people of Judah and Jerusalem, ‘You must worship at this altar.’
net.ISA_36_8: Now make a deal with my master the king of Assyria, and I will give you 2,000 horses, provided you can find enough riders for them.
net.ISA_36_9: Certainly you will not refuse one of my master’s minor officials and trust in Egypt for chariots and horsemen.
net.ISA_36_10: Furthermore it was by the command of the Lord that I marched up against this land to destroy it. The Lord told me, ‘March up against this land and destroy it!’”’”
net.ISA_36_11: Eliakim, Shebna, and Joah said to the chief adviser, “Speak to your servants in Aramaic, for we understand it. Don’t speak with us in the Judahite dialect in the hearing of the people who are on the wall.”
net.ISA_36_12: But the chief adviser said, “My master did not send me to speak these words only to your master and to you. His message is also for the men who sit on the wall, for they will eat their own excrement and drink their own urine along with you!”
net.ISA_36_13: The chief adviser then stood there and called out loudly in the Judahite dialect, “Listen to the message of the great king, the king of Assyria.
net.ISA_36_14: This is what the king says: ‘Don’t let Hezekiah mislead you, for he is not able to rescue you!
net.ISA_36_15: Don’t let Hezekiah talk you into trusting in the Lord by saying, “The Lord will certainly rescue us; this city will not be handed over to the king of Assyria.”
net.ISA_36_16: Don’t listen to Hezekiah!’ For this is what the king of Assyria says, ‘Send me a token of your submission and surrender to me. Then each of you may eat from his own vine and fig tree and drink water from his own cistern,
net.ISA_36_17: until I come and take you to a land just like your own—a land of grain and new wine, a land of bread and vineyards.
net.ISA_36_18: Hezekiah is misleading you when he says, “The Lord will rescue us.” Have any of the gods of the nations rescued their lands from the power of the king of Assyria?
net.ISA_36_19: Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim? Indeed, did any gods rescue Samaria from my power?
net.ISA_36_20: Who among all the gods of these lands have rescued their lands from my power? So how can the Lord rescue Jerusalem from my power?’”
net.ISA_36_21: They were silent and did not respond, for the king had ordered, “Don’t respond to him.”
net.ISA_36_22: Eliakim son of Hilkiah, the palace supervisor, accompanied by Shebna the scribe and Joah son of Asaph, the secretary, went to Hezekiah with their clothes torn and reported to him what the chief adviser had said.
net.ISA_37_1: When King Hezekiah heard this, he tore his clothes, put on sackcloth, and went to the Lord’s temple.
net.ISA_37_2: Eliakim the palace supervisor, Shebna the scribe, and the leading priests, clothed in sackcloth, sent this message to the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz:
net.ISA_37_3: “This is what Hezekiah says: ‘This is a day of distress, insults, and humiliation, as when a baby is ready to leave the birth canal, but the mother lacks the strength to push it through.
net.ISA_37_4: Perhaps the Lord your God will hear all these things the chief adviser has spoken on behalf of his master, the king of Assyria, who sent him to taunt the living God. When the Lord your God hears, perhaps he will punish him for the things he has said. So pray for this remnant that remains.’”
net.ISA_37_5: When King Hezekiah’s servants came to Isaiah,
net.ISA_37_6: Isaiah said to them, “Tell your master this: ‘This is what the Lord has said: “Don’t be afraid because of the things you have heard—these insults the king of Assyria’s servants have hurled against me.
net.ISA_37_7: Look, I will take control of his mind; he will receive a report and return to his own land. I will cut him down with a sword in his own land.”’”
net.ISA_37_8: When the chief adviser heard the king of Assyria had departed from Lachish, he left and went to Libnah, where the king was campaigning.
net.ISA_37_9: The king heard that King Tirhakah of Ethiopia was marching out to fight him. He again sent messengers to Hezekiah, ordering them:
net.ISA_37_10: “Tell King Hezekiah of Judah this: ‘Don’t let your God in whom you trust mislead you when he says, “Jerusalem will not be handed over to the king of Assyria.”
net.ISA_37_11: Certainly you have heard how the kings of Assyria have annihilated all lands. Do you really think you will be rescued?
net.ISA_37_12: Were the nations whom my predecessors destroyed—the nations of Gozan, Haran, Rezeph, and the people of Eden in Telassar—rescued by their gods?
net.ISA_37_13: Where is the king of Hamath or the king of Arpad or the kings of Lair, Sepharvaim, Hena, and Ivvah?’”
net.ISA_37_14: Hezekiah took the letter from the messengers and read it. Then Hezekiah went up to the Lord’s temple and spread it out before the Lord.
net.ISA_37_16: “O Lord of Heaven’s Armies, O God of Israel, who is enthroned on the cherubim! You alone are God over all the kingdoms of the earth. You made the sky and the earth.
net.ISA_37_17: Pay attention, Lord, and hear! Open your eyes, Lord, and observe! Listen to this entire message Sennacherib sent and how he taunts the living God!
net.ISA_37_18: It is true, Lord, that the kings of Assyria have destroyed all the nations and their lands.
net.ISA_37_19: They have burned the gods of the nations, for they are not really gods, but only the product of human hands manufactured from wood and stone. That is why the Assyrians could destroy them.
net.ISA_37_20: Now, O Lord our God, rescue us from his power, so all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you alone are the Lord.”
net.ISA_37_21: Isaiah son of Amoz sent this message to Hezekiah: “This is what the Lord God of Israel has said: ‘As to what you have prayed to me concerning King Sennacherib of Assyria,
net.ISA_37_22: this is what the Lord says about him: “‘The virgin daughter Zion despises you—she makes fun of you; daughter Jerusalem shakes her head after you.
net.ISA_37_23: Whom have you taunted and hurled insults at? At whom have you shouted and looked so arrogantly? At the Holy One of Israel!
net.ISA_37_24: Through your messengers you taunted the Lord, “With my many chariots I climbed up the high mountains, the slopes of Lebanon. I cut down its tall cedars and its best evergreens. I invaded its remotest regions, its thickest woods.
net.ISA_37_25: I dug wells and drank water. With the soles of my feet I dried up all the rivers of Egypt.”’
net.ISA_37_26: Certainly you must have heard! Long ago I worked it out, in ancient times I planned it, and now I am bringing it to pass. The plan is this: Fortified cities will crash into heaps of ruins.
net.ISA_37_27: Their residents are powerless; they are terrified and ashamed. They are as short-lived as plants in the field or green vegetation. They are as short-lived as grass on the rooftops when it is scorched by the east wind.
net.ISA_37_28: I know where you live and everything you do and how you rage against me.
net.ISA_37_29: Because you rage against me and the uproar you create has reached my ears, I will put my hook in your nose, and my bit between your lips, and I will lead you back the way you came.’
net.ISA_37_30: “This will be your reminder that I have spoken the truth: This year you will eat what grows wild, and next year what grows on its own. But the year after that you will plant seed and harvest crops; you will plant vines and consume their produce.
net.ISA_37_31: Those who remain in Judah will take root in the ground and bear fruit.
net.ISA_37_32: “For a remnant will leave Jerusalem; survivors will come out of Mount Zion. The zeal of the Lord of Heaven’s Armies will accomplish this.
net.ISA_37_33: So this is what the Lord says about the king of Assyria: “‘He will not enter this city, nor will he shoot an arrow here. He will not attack it with his shielded warriors, nor will he build siege works against it.
net.ISA_37_34: He will go back the way he came— he will not enter this city,’ says the Lord.
net.ISA_37_35: I will shield this city and rescue it for the sake of my reputation and because of my promise to David my servant.”
net.ISA_37_36: The angel of the Lord went out and killed 185,000 troops in the Assyrian camp. When they got up early the next morning, there were all the corpses!
net.ISA_37_37: So King Sennacherib of Assyria broke camp and went on his way. He went home and stayed in Nineveh.
net.ISA_37_38: One day, as he was worshiping in the temple of his god Nisroch, his sons Adrammelech and Sharezer struck him down with the sword. They ran away to the land of Ararat; his son Esarhaddon replaced him as king.
net.ISA_38_1: In those days Hezekiah was stricken with a terminal illness. The prophet Isaiah son of Amoz visited him and told him, “This is what the Lord says, ‘Give instructions to your household, for you are about to die; you will not get well.’”
net.ISA_38_2: Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and prayed to the Lord,
net.ISA_38_3: “Please, Lord. Remember how I have served you faithfully and with wholehearted devotion, and how I have carried out your will.” Then Hezekiah wept bitterly.
net.ISA_38_4: The Lord’s message came to Isaiah,
net.ISA_38_5: “Go and tell Hezekiah: ‘This is what the Lord God of your ancestor David says: “I have heard your prayer; I have seen your tears. Look, I will add fifteen years to your life.
net.ISA_38_6: I will also rescue you and this city from the king of Assyria. I will shield this city.”’”
net.ISA_38_7: Isaiah replied, “This is your sign from the Lord confirming that the Lord will do what he has said:
net.ISA_38_8: Look, I will make the shadow go back ten steps on the stairs of Ahaz.” And then the shadow went back ten steps. Hezekiah’s Song of Thanks
net.ISA_38_9: This is the prayer of King Hezekiah of Judah when he was sick and then recovered from his illness:
net.ISA_38_10: “I thought, ‘In the middle of my life I must walk through the gates of Sheol, I am deprived of the rest of my years.’
net.ISA_38_11: “I thought, ‘I will no longer see the Lord in the land of the living, I will no longer look on humankind with the inhabitants of the world.
net.ISA_38_12: My dwelling place is removed and taken away from me as a shepherd’s tent. I rolled up my life like a weaver rolls cloth; from the loom he cuts me off. You turn day into night and end my life.
net.ISA_38_13: I cry out until morning; like a lion he shatters all my bones; you turn day into night and end my life.
net.ISA_38_14: Like a swallow or a thrush I chirp, I coo like a dove; my eyes grow tired from looking up to the sky. O Lord, I am oppressed; help me!
net.ISA_38_15: What can I say? He has decreed and acted. I will walk slowly all my years because I am overcome with grief.
net.ISA_38_16: O Lord, your decrees can give men life; may years of life be restored to me. Restore my health and preserve my life.’
net.ISA_38_17: “Look, the grief I experienced was for my benefit. You delivered me from the Pit of oblivion. For you removed all my sins from your sight.
net.ISA_38_18: Indeed Sheol does not give you thanks; death does not praise you. Those who descend into the Pit do not anticipate your faithfulness.
net.ISA_38_19: The living person, the living person, he gives you thanks, as I do today. A father tells his sons about your faithfulness.
net.ISA_38_20: The Lord is about to deliver me, and we will celebrate with music for the rest of our lives in the Lord’s temple.”
net.ISA_38_21: (Isaiah ordered, “Let them take a fig cake and apply it to the ulcerated sore and he will get well.”
net.ISA_38_22: Hezekiah said, “What is the confirming sign that I will go up to the Lord’s temple?”)
net.ISA_39_1: At that time Merodach Baladan son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a gift to Hezekiah, for he heard that Hezekiah had been ill and had recovered.
net.ISA_39_2: Hezekiah welcomed them and showed them his storehouse with its silver, gold, spices, and high-quality olive oil, as well as his whole armory and everything in his treasuries. Hezekiah showed them everything in his palace and in his whole kingdom.
net.ISA_39_3: Isaiah the prophet visited King Hezekiah and asked him, “What did these men say? Where do they come from?” Hezekiah replied, “They come from the distant land of Babylon.”
net.ISA_39_4: Isaiah asked, “What have they seen in your palace?” Hezekiah replied, “They have seen everything in my palace. I showed them everything in my treasuries.”
net.ISA_39_5: Isaiah said to Hezekiah, “Listen to the message of the Lord of Heaven’s Armies:
net.ISA_39_6: ‘Look, a time is coming when everything in your palace and the things your ancestors have accumulated to this day will be carried away to Babylon; nothing will be left,’ says the Lord.
net.ISA_39_7: ‘Some of your very own descendants whom you father will be taken away and will be made eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.’”
net.ISA_39_8: Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “The Lord’s message that you have announced is appropriate.” Then he thought, “For there will be peace and stability during my lifetime.”
net.ISA_40_1: “Comfort, comfort my people,” says your God.
net.ISA_40_2: “Speak kindly to Jerusalem and tell her that her time of warfare is over, that her punishment is completed. For the Lord has made her pay double for all her sins.”
net.ISA_40_3: A voice cries out, “In the wilderness clear a way for the Lord; build a level road through the rift valley for our God.
net.ISA_40_4: Every valley must be elevated, and every mountain and hill leveled. The rough terrain will become a level plain, the rugged landscape a wide valley.
net.ISA_40_5: The splendor of the Lord will be revealed, and all people will see it at the same time. For the Lord has decreed it.”
net.ISA_40_6: A voice says, “Cry out!” Another asks, “What should I cry out?” The first voice responds: “All people are like grass, and all their promises are like the flowers in the field.
net.ISA_40_7: The grass dries up, the flowers wither, when the wind sent by the Lord blows on them. Surely humanity is like grass.
net.ISA_40_8: The grass dries up, the flowers wither, but the decree of our God is forever reliable.”
net.ISA_40_9: Go up on a high mountain, O herald Zion. Shout out loudly, O herald Jerusalem! Shout, don’t be afraid! Say to the towns of Judah, “Here is your God!”
net.ISA_40_10: Look, the Sovereign Lord comes as a victorious warrior; his military power establishes his rule. Look, his reward is with him; his prize goes before him.
net.ISA_40_11: Like a shepherd he tends his flock; he gathers up the lambs with his arm; he carries them close to his heart; he leads the ewes along. The Lord is Incomparable
net.ISA_40_12: Who has measured out the waters in the hollow of his hand, or carefully measured the sky, or carefully weighed the soil of the earth, or weighed the mountains in a balance, or the hills on scales?
net.ISA_40_13: Who comprehends the mind of the Lord, or gives him instruction as his counselor?
net.ISA_40_14: From whom does he receive directions? Who teaches him the correct way to do things, or imparts knowledge to him, or instructs him in skillful design?
net.ISA_40_15: Look, the nations are like a drop in a bucket; they are regarded as dust on the scales. He lifts the coastlands as if they were dust.
net.ISA_40_16: Not even Lebanon could supply enough firewood for a sacrifice; its wild animals would not provide enough burnt offerings.
net.ISA_40_17: All the nations are insignificant before him; they are regarded as absolutely nothing.
net.ISA_40_18: To whom can you compare God? To what image can you liken him?
net.ISA_40_19: A craftsman casts an idol; a metalsmith overlays it with gold and forges silver chains for it.
net.ISA_40_20: To make a contribution one selects wood that will not rot; he then seeks a skilled craftsman to make an idol that will not fall over.
net.ISA_40_21: Do you not know? Do you not hear? Has it not been told to you since the very beginning? Have you not understood from the time the earth’s foundations were made?
net.ISA_40_22: He is the one who sits on the earth’s horizon; its inhabitants are like grasshoppers before him. He is the one who stretches out the sky like a thin curtain, and spreads it out like a pitched tent.
net.ISA_40_23: He is the one who reduces rulers to nothing; he makes the earth’s leaders insignificant.
net.ISA_40_24: Indeed, they are barely planted; yes, they are barely sown; yes, they barely take root in the earth, and then he blows on them, causing them to dry up, and the wind carries them away like straw.
net.ISA_40_25: “To whom can you compare me? Whom do I resemble?” says the Holy One.
net.ISA_40_26: Look up at the sky! Who created all these heavenly lights? He is the one who leads out their ranks; he calls them all by name. Because of his absolute power and awesome strength, not one of them is missing.
net.ISA_40_27: Why do you say, Jacob, Why do you say, Israel, “The Lord is not aware of what is happening to me; My God is not concerned with my vindication”?
net.ISA_40_28: Do you not know? Have you not heard? The Lord is an eternal God, the Creator of the whole earth. He does not get tired or weary; there is no limit to his wisdom.
net.ISA_40_29: He gives strength to those who are tired; to the ones who lack power, he gives renewed energy.
net.ISA_40_30: Even youths get tired and weary; even strong young men clumsily stumble.
net.ISA_40_31: But those who wait for the Lord’s help find renewed strength; they rise up as if they had eagles’ wings, they run without growing weary, they walk without getting tired.
net.ISA_41_1: “Listen to me in silence, you coastlands! Let the nations find renewed strength! Let them approach and then speak; let us come together for debate.
net.ISA_41_2: Who stirs up this one from the east? Who officially commissions him for service? He hands nations over to him, and enables him to subdue kings. He makes them like dust with his sword, like windblown straw with his bow.
net.ISA_41_3: He pursues them and passes by unharmed; he advances with great speed.
net.ISA_41_4: Who acts and carries out decrees? Who summons the successive generations from the beginning? I, the Lord, am present at the very beginning, and at the very end—I am the one.
net.ISA_41_5: The coastlands see and are afraid; the whole earth trembles; they approach and come.
net.ISA_41_6: They help one another; one says to the other, ‘Be strong!’
net.ISA_41_7: The craftsman encourages the metalsmith, the one who wields the hammer encourages the one who pounds on the anvil. He approves the quality of the welding, and nails it down so it won’t fall over. The Lord Encourages His People
net.ISA_41_8: “You, my servant Israel, Jacob, whom I have chosen, offspring of Abraham my friend,
net.ISA_41_9: you whom I am bringing back from the earth’s extremities, and have summoned from the remote regions— I told you, ‘You are my servant.’ I have chosen you and not rejected you.
net.ISA_41_10: Don’t be afraid, for I am with you! Don’t be frightened, for I am your God! I strengthen you— yes, I help you— yes, I uphold you with my victorious right hand!
net.ISA_41_11: Look, all who were angry at you will be ashamed and humiliated; your adversaries will be reduced to nothing and perish.
net.ISA_41_12: When you will look for your opponents, you will not find them; your enemies will be reduced to absolutely nothing.
net.ISA_41_13: For I am the Lord your God, the one who takes hold of your right hand, who says to you, ‘Don’t be afraid, I am helping you.’
net.ISA_41_14: Don’t be afraid, despised insignificant Jacob, men of Israel. I am helping you,” says the Lord, your Protector, the Holy One of Israel.
net.ISA_41_15: “Look, I am making you like a sharp threshing sledge, new and double-edged. You will thresh the mountains and crush them; you will make the hills like straw.
net.ISA_41_16: You will winnow them and the wind will blow them away; the wind will scatter them. You will rejoice in the Lord; you will boast in the Holy One of Israel.
net.ISA_41_17: The oppressed and the poor look for water, but there is none; their tongues are parched from thirst. I, the Lord, will respond to their prayers; I, the God of Israel, will not abandon them.
net.ISA_41_18: I will make streams flow down the slopes and produce springs in the middle of the valleys. I will turn the wilderness into a pool of water and the arid land into springs.
net.ISA_41_19: I will make cedars, acacias, myrtles, and olive trees grow in the wilderness; I will make evergreens, firs, and cypresses grow together in the arid rift valley.
net.ISA_41_20: I will do this so people will observe and recognize, so they will pay attention and understand that the Lord’s power has accomplished this, and that the Holy One of Israel has brought it into being. The Lord Challenges the Pagan Gods
net.ISA_41_21: “Present your argument,” says the Lord. “Produce your evidence,” says Jacob’s king.
net.ISA_41_22: “Let them produce evidence! Let them tell us what will happen! Tell us about your earlier predictive oracles, so we may examine them and see how they were fulfilled. Or decree for us some future events!
net.ISA_41_23: Predict how future events will turn out, so we might know you are gods. Yes, do something good or something bad, so we might be frightened and in awe.
net.ISA_41_24: Look, you are nothing, and your accomplishments are nonexistent; the one who chooses to worship you is disgusting.
net.ISA_41_25: I have stirred up one out of the north and he advances, one from the eastern horizon who prays in my name. He steps on rulers as if they were clay, like a potter treading the clay.
net.ISA_41_26: Who decreed this from the beginning, so we could know? Who announced it ahead of time, so we could say, ‘He’s correct’? Indeed, none of them decreed it. Indeed, none of them announced it. Indeed, no one heard you say anything!
net.ISA_41_27: I first decreed to Zion, ‘Look, here’s what will happen!’ I sent a herald to Jerusalem.
net.ISA_41_28: I look, but there is no one, among them there is no one who serves as an adviser, that I might ask questions and receive answers.
net.ISA_41_29: Look, all of them are nothing, their accomplishments are nonexistent; their metal images lack any real substance.
net.ISA_42_1: “Here is my servant whom I support, my chosen one in whom I take pleasure. I have placed my Spirit on him; he will make just decrees for the nations.
net.ISA_42_2: He will not cry out or shout; he will not publicize himself in the streets.
net.ISA_42_3: A crushed reed he will not break, a dim wick he will not extinguish; he will faithfully make just decrees.
net.ISA_42_4: He will not grow dim or be crushed before establishing justice on the earth; the coastlands will wait in anticipation for his decrees.”
net.ISA_42_5: This is what the true God, the Lord, says— the one who created the sky and stretched it out, the one who fashioned the earth and everything that lives on it, the one who gives breath to the people on it, and life to those who live on it:
net.ISA_42_6: “I, the Lord, officially commission you; I take hold of your hand. I protect you and make you a covenant mediator for people, and a light to the nations,
net.ISA_42_7: to open blind eyes, to release prisoners from dungeons, those who live in darkness from prisons. The Lord Intervenes
net.ISA_42_8: “I am the Lord! That is my name! I will not share my glory with anyone else, or the praise due me with idols.
net.ISA_42_9: Look, my earlier predictive oracles have come to pass; now I announce new events. Before they begin to occur, I reveal them to you.”
net.ISA_42_10: Sing to the Lord a brand new song! Praise him from the horizon of the earth, you who go down to the sea, and everything that lives in it, you coastlands and those who live there.
net.ISA_42_11: Let the wilderness and its cities shout out, the towns where the nomads of Kedar live. Let the residents of Sela shout joyfully; let them shout loudly from the mountaintops.
net.ISA_42_12: Let them give the Lord the honor he deserves; let them praise his deeds in the coastlands.
net.ISA_42_13: The Lord emerges like a hero, like a warrior he inspires himself for battle; he shouts, yes, he yells, he shows his enemies his power.
net.ISA_42_14: “I have been inactive for a long time; I kept quiet and held back. Like a woman in labor I groan; I pant and gasp.
net.ISA_42_15: I will make the trees on the mountains and hills wither up; I will dry up all their vegetation. I will turn streams into islands, and dry up pools of water.
net.ISA_42_16: I will lead the blind along an unfamiliar way; I will guide them down paths they have never traveled. I will turn the darkness in front of them into light, and level out the rough ground. This is what I will do for them. I will not abandon them.
net.ISA_42_17: Those who trust in idols will turn back and be utterly humiliated, those who say to metal images, ‘You are our gods.’ The Lord Reasons with His People
net.ISA_42_18: “Listen, you deaf ones! Take notice, you blind ones!
net.ISA_42_19: My servant is truly blind, my messenger is truly deaf. My covenant partner, the servant of the Lord, is truly blind.
net.ISA_42_20: You see many things, but don’t comprehend; their ears are open, but do not hear.”
net.ISA_42_21: The Lord wanted to exhibit his justice by magnifying his law and displaying it.
net.ISA_42_22: But these people are looted and plundered; all of them are trapped in pits and held captive in prisons. They were carried away as loot with no one to rescue them; they were carried away as plunder, and no one says, “Bring that back!”
net.ISA_42_23: Who among you will pay attention to this? Who will listen attentively in the future?
net.ISA_42_24: Who handed Jacob over to the robber? Who handed Israel over to the looters? Was it not the Lord, against whom we sinned? They refused to follow his commands; they disobeyed his law.
net.ISA_42_25: So he poured out his fierce anger on them, along with the devastation of war. Its flames encircled them, but they did not realize it; it burned against them, but they did not take it to heart.
net.ISA_43_1: Now, this is what the Lord says, the one who created you, O Jacob, and formed you, O Israel: “Don’t be afraid, for I will protect you. I call you by name, you are mine.
net.ISA_43_2: When you pass through the waters, I am with you; when you pass through the streams, they will not overwhelm you. When you walk through the fire, you will not be burned; the flames will not harm you.
net.ISA_43_3: For I am the Lord your God, the Holy One of Israel, your deliverer. I have handed over Egypt as a ransom price, Ethiopia and Seba in place of you.
net.ISA_43_4: Since you are precious and special in my sight, and I love you, I will hand over people in place of you, nations in place of your life.
net.ISA_43_5: Don’t be afraid, for I am with you. From the east I will bring your descendants; from the west I will gather you.
net.ISA_43_6: I will say to the north, ‘Hand them over!’ and to the south, ‘Don’t hold any back!’ Bring my sons from distant lands, and my daughters from the remote regions of the earth,
net.ISA_43_7: everyone who belongs to me, whom I created for my glory, whom I formed—yes, whom I made. The Lord Declares His Sovereignty
net.ISA_43_8: Bring out the people who are blind, even though they have eyes, those who are deaf, even though they have ears!
net.ISA_43_9: All nations gather together, the peoples assemble. Who among them announced this? Who predicted earlier events for us? Let them produce their witnesses to testify they were right; let them listen and affirm, “It is true.”
net.ISA_43_10: You are my witnesses,” says the Lord, “my servant whom I have chosen, so that you may consider and believe in me, and understand that I am he. No god was formed before me, and none will outlive me.
net.ISA_43_11: I, I am the Lord, and there is no deliverer besides me.
net.ISA_43_12: I decreed and delivered and proclaimed, and there was no other god among you. You are my witnesses,” says the Lord, “that I am God.
net.ISA_43_13: From this day forward I am he; no one can deliver from my power; I will act, and who can prevent it?” The Lord Will Do Something New
net.ISA_43_14: This is what the Lord says, your Protector, the Holy One of Israel: “For your sake I send to Babylon and make them all fugitives, turning the Babylonians’ joyful shouts into mourning songs.
net.ISA_43_15: I am the Lord, your Holy One, the one who created Israel, your king.”
net.ISA_43_16: This is what the Lord says, the one who made a road through the sea, a pathway through the surging waters,
net.ISA_43_17: the one who led chariots and horses to destruction, together with a mighty army. They fell down, never to rise again; they were extinguished, put out like a burning wick:
net.ISA_43_18: “Don’t remember these earlier events; don’t recall these former events.
net.ISA_43_19: Look, I am about to do something new. Now it begins to happen! Do you not recognize it? Yes, I will make a road in the wilderness and paths in the wastelands.
net.ISA_43_20: The wild animals honor me, the jackals and ostriches, because I put water in the wilderness and streams in the wastelands, to quench the thirst of my chosen people,
net.ISA_43_21: the people whom I formed for myself, so they might praise me. The Lord Rebukes His People
net.ISA_43_22: “But you did not call for me, O Jacob; you did not long for me, O Israel.
net.ISA_43_23: You did not bring me lambs for your burnt offerings; you did not honor me with your sacrifices. I did not burden you with offerings; I did not make you weary by demanding incense.
net.ISA_43_24: You did not buy me aromatic reeds; you did not present to me the fat of your sacrifices. Yet you burdened me with your sins; you made me weary with your evil deeds.
net.ISA_43_25: I, I am the one who blots out your rebellious deeds for my sake; your sins I do not remember.
net.ISA_43_26: Remind me of what happened. Let’s debate! You, prove to me that you are right!
net.ISA_43_27: The father of your nation sinned; your spokesmen rebelled against me.
net.ISA_43_28: So I defiled your holy princes, and handed Jacob over to destruction, and subjected Israel to humiliating abuse.
net.ISA_44_1: “Now, listen, Jacob my servant, Israel whom I have chosen!”
net.ISA_44_2: This is what the Lord, the one who made you, says— the one who formed you in the womb and helps you: “Don’t be afraid, my servant Jacob, Jeshurun, whom I have chosen.
net.ISA_44_3: For I will pour water on the parched ground and cause streams to flow on the dry land. I will pour my Spirit on your offspring and my blessing on your children.
net.ISA_44_4: They will sprout up like a tree in the grass, like poplars beside channels of water.
net.ISA_44_5: One will say, ‘I belong to the Lord,’ and another will use the name ‘Jacob.’ One will write on his hand, ‘The Lord’s,’ and use the name ‘Israel.’” The Absurdity of Idolatry
net.ISA_44_6: This is what the Lord, Israel’s king, says, their Protector, the Lord of Heaven’s Armies: “I am the first and I am the last, there is no God but me.
net.ISA_44_7: Who is like me? Let him make his claim! Let him announce it and explain it to me— since I established an ancient people — let them announce future events.
net.ISA_44_8: Don’t panic! Don’t be afraid! Did I not tell you beforehand and decree it? You are my witnesses! Is there any God but me? There is no other sheltering rock; I know of none.
net.ISA_44_9: All who form idols are nothing; the things in which they delight are worthless. Their witnesses cannot see; they recognize nothing, so they are put to shame.
net.ISA_44_10: Who forms a god and casts an idol that will prove worthless?
net.ISA_44_11: Look, all his associates will be put to shame; the craftsmen are mere humans. Let them all assemble and take their stand. They will panic and be put to shame.
net.ISA_44_12: A blacksmith works with his tool and forges metal over the coals. He forms it with hammers; he makes it with his strong arm. He gets hungry and loses his energy; he drinks no water and gets tired.
net.ISA_44_13: A carpenter takes measurements; he marks out an outline of its form; he scrapes it with chisels, and marks it with a compass. He patterns it after the human form, like a well-built human being, and puts it in a shrine.
net.ISA_44_14: He cuts down cedars and acquires a cypress or an oak. He gets trees from the forest; he plants a cedar and the rain makes it grow.
net.ISA_44_15: A man uses it to make a fire; he takes some of it and warms himself. Yes, he kindles a fire and bakes bread. Then he makes a god and worships it; he makes an idol and bows down to it.
net.ISA_44_16: Half of it he burns in the fire— over that half he cooks meat; he roasts a meal and fills himself. Yes, he warms himself and says, ‘Ah! I am warm as I look at the fire.’
net.ISA_44_17: With the rest of it he makes a god, his idol; he bows down to it and worships it. He prays to it, saying, ‘Rescue me, for you are my god!’
net.ISA_44_18: They do not comprehend or understand, for their eyes are blind and cannot see; their minds do not discern.
net.ISA_44_19: No one thinks to himself, nor do they comprehend or understand and say to themselves: ‘I burned half of it in the fire— yes, I baked bread over the coals; I roasted meat and ate it. With the rest of it should I make a disgusting idol? Should I bow down to dry wood?’
net.ISA_44_20: He feeds on ashes; his deceived mind misleads him. He cannot rescue himself, nor does he say, ‘Is this not a false god I hold in my right hand?’
net.ISA_44_21: Remember these things, O Jacob, O Israel, for you are my servant. I formed you to be my servant; O Israel, I will not forget you!
net.ISA_44_22: I remove the guilt of your rebellious deeds as if they were a cloud, the guilt of your sins as if they were a cloud. Come back to me, for I protect you.”
net.ISA_44_23: Shout for joy, O sky, for the Lord intervenes; shout out, you subterranean regions of the earth. O mountains, give a joyful shout; you too, O forest and all your trees! For the Lord protects Jacob; he reveals his splendor through Israel. The Lord Empowers Cyrus
net.ISA_44_24: This is what the Lord, your Protector, says, the one who formed you in the womb: “I am the Lord, who made everything, who alone stretched out the sky, who fashioned the earth all by myself,
net.ISA_44_25: who frustrates the omens of the empty talkers and humiliates the omen readers, who overturns the counsel of the wise men and makes their advice seem foolish,
net.ISA_44_26: who fulfills the oracles of his prophetic servants and brings to pass the announcements of his messengers, who says about Jerusalem, ‘She will be inhabited,’ and about the towns of Judah, ‘They will be rebuilt, her ruins I will raise up,’
net.ISA_44_27: who says to the deep sea, ‘Be dry! I will dry up your sea currents,’
net.ISA_44_28: who commissions Cyrus, the one I appointed as shepherd to carry out all my wishes and to decree concerning Jerusalem, ‘She will be rebuilt,’ and concerning the temple, ‘It will be reconstructed.’
net.ISA_45_1: “This is what the Lord says to his chosen one, to Cyrus, whose right hand I hold in order to subdue nations before him, and disarm kings, to open doors before him, so gates remain unclosed:
net.ISA_45_2: ‘I will go before you and level mountains. Bronze doors I will shatter and iron bars I will hack through.
net.ISA_45_3: I will give you hidden treasures, riches stashed away in secret places, so you may recognize that I am the Lord, the one who calls you by name, the God of Israel.
net.ISA_45_4: For the sake of my servant Jacob, Israel, my chosen one, I call you by name and give you a title of respect, even though you do not submit to me.
net.ISA_45_5: I am the Lord, I have no peer, there is no God but me. I arm you for battle, even though you do not recognize me.
net.ISA_45_6: I do this so people will recognize from east to west that there is no God but me; I am the Lord, I have no peer.
net.ISA_45_7: I am the one who forms light and creates darkness; the one who brings about peace and creates calamity. I am the Lord, who accomplishes all these things.
net.ISA_45_8: O sky, rain down from above! Let the clouds send down showers of deliverance! Let the earth absorb it so salvation may grow, and deliverance may sprout up along with it. I, the Lord, create it.’” The Lord Gives a Warning
net.ISA_45_9: One who argues with his Creator is in grave danger, one who is like a mere shard among the other shards on the ground! The clay should not say to the potter, “What in the world are you doing? Your work lacks skill!”
net.ISA_45_10: Danger awaits one who says to his father, “What in the world are you fathering?” and to his mother, “What in the world are you bringing forth?”
net.ISA_45_11: This is what the Lord says, the Holy One of Israel, the one who formed him, concerning things to come: “How dare you question me about my children! How dare you tell me what to do with the work of my own hands!
net.ISA_45_12: I made the earth; I created the people who live on it. It was me—my hands stretched out the sky. I give orders to all the heavenly lights.
net.ISA_45_13: It is me—I stir him up and commission him; I will make all his ways level. He will rebuild my city; he will send my exiled people home, but not for a price or a bribe,” says the Lord of Heaven’s Armies. The Lord is the Nations’ Only Hope
net.ISA_45_14: This is what the Lord says: “The profit of Egypt and the revenue of Ethiopia, along with the Sabeans, those tall men, will be brought to you and become yours. They will walk behind you, coming along in chains. They will bow down to you and pray to you: ‘Truly God is with you; he has no peer; there is no other God!’”
net.ISA_45_15: Yes, you are a God who keeps hidden, O God of Israel, deliverer!
net.ISA_45_16: They will all be ashamed and embarrassed; those who fashion idols will all be humiliated.
net.ISA_45_17: Israel will be delivered once and for all by the Lord; you will never again be ashamed or humiliated.
net.ISA_45_18: For this is what the Lord says, the one who created the sky— he is the true God, the one who formed the earth and made it; he established it, he did not create it without order, he formed it to be inhabited: “I am the Lord, I have no peer.
net.ISA_45_19: I have not spoken in secret, in some hidden place. I did not tell Jacob’s descendants, ‘Seek me in vain!’ I am the Lord, the one who speaks honestly, who makes reliable announcements.
net.ISA_45_20: Gather together and come! Approach together, you refugees from the nations. Those who carry wooden idols know nothing, those who pray to a god that cannot deliver.
net.ISA_45_21: Tell me! Present the evidence! Let them consult with one another. Who predicted this in the past? Who announced it beforehand? Was it not I, the Lord? I have no peer, there is no God but me, a God who vindicates and delivers; there is none but me.
net.ISA_45_22: Turn to me so you can be delivered, all you who live in the earth’s remote regions! For I am God, and I have no peer.
net.ISA_45_23: I solemnly make this oath — what I say is true and reliable: ‘Surely every knee will bow to me, every tongue will solemnly affirm;
net.ISA_45_24: they will say about me, “Yes, the Lord is a powerful deliverer.”’” All who are angry at him will cower before him.
net.ISA_45_25: All the descendants of Israel will be vindicated by the Lord and will boast in him.
net.ISA_46_1: Bel kneels down, Nebo bends low. Their images weigh down animals and beasts. Your heavy images are burdensome to tired animals.
net.ISA_46_2: Together they bend low and kneel down; they are unable to rescue the images; they themselves head off into captivity.
net.ISA_46_3: “Listen to me, O family of Jacob, all you who are left from the family of Israel, you who have been carried from birth, you who have been supported from the time you left the womb.
net.ISA_46_4: Even when you are old, I will take care of you, even when you have gray hair, I will carry you. I made you and I will support you; I will carry you and rescue you.
net.ISA_46_5: To whom can you compare and liken me? Tell me whom you think I resemble, so we can be compared!
net.ISA_46_6: Those who empty out gold from a purse and weigh out silver on the scale hire a metalsmith, who makes it into a god. They then bow down and worship it.
net.ISA_46_7: They put it on their shoulder and carry it; they put it in its place and it just stands there; it does not move from its place. Even when someone cries out to it, it does not reply; it does not deliver him from his distress.
net.ISA_46_8: Remember this, so you can be brave. Think about it, you rebels!
net.ISA_46_9: Remember what I accomplished in antiquity. Truly I am God, I have no peer; I am God, and there is none like me,
net.ISA_46_10: who announces the end from the beginning and reveals beforehand what has not yet occurred; who says, ‘My plan will be realized, I will accomplish what I desire;’
net.ISA_46_11: who summons an eagle from the east, from a distant land, one who carries out my plan. Yes, I have decreed, yes, I will bring it to pass; I have formulated a plan, yes, I will carry it out.
net.ISA_46_12: Listen to me, you stubborn people, you who distance yourselves from doing what is right.
net.ISA_46_13: I am bringing my deliverance near, it is not far away; I am bringing my salvation near, it does not wait. I will save Zion; I will adorn Israel with my splendor.
net.ISA_47_1: “Fall down! Sit in the dirt, O virgin daughter Babylon! Sit on the ground, not on a throne, O daughter of the Babylonians! Indeed, you will no longer be called delicate and pampered.
net.ISA_47_2: Pick up millstones and grind flour. Remove your veil, strip off your skirt, expose your legs, cross the streams.
net.ISA_47_3: Let your naked body be exposed. Your shame will be on display! I will get revenge; I will not have pity on anyone,”
net.ISA_47_4: says our Protector— the Lord of Heaven’s Armies is his name, the Holy One of Israel.
net.ISA_47_5: “Sit silently! Go to a hiding place, O daughter of the Babylonians! Indeed, you will no longer be called ‘Queen of kingdoms.’
net.ISA_47_6: I was angry at my people; I defiled my special possession and handed them over to you. You showed them no mercy; you even placed a very heavy burden on old people.
net.ISA_47_7: You said, ‘I will rule forever as permanent queen!’ You did not think about these things; you did not consider how it would turn out.
net.ISA_47_8: So now, listen to this, O one who lives so lavishly, who lives securely, who says to herself, ‘I am unique! No one can compare to me! I will never have to live as a widow; I will never lose my children.’
net.ISA_47_9: Both of these will come upon you suddenly, in one day! You will lose your children and be widowed. You will be overwhelmed by these tragedies, despite your many incantations and your numerous amulets.
net.ISA_47_10: You were complacent in your evil deeds; you thought, ‘No one sees me.’ Your self-professed wisdom and knowledge lead you astray, when you say, ‘I am unique! No one can compare to me!’
net.ISA_47_11: Disaster will overtake you; you will not know how to charm it away. Destruction will fall on you; you will not be able to appease it. Calamity will strike you suddenly, before you recognize it.
net.ISA_47_12: Persist in trusting your amulets and your many incantations, which you have faithfully recited since your youth! Maybe you will be successful — maybe you will scare away disaster.
net.ISA_47_13: You are tired out from listening to so much advice. Let them take their stand— the ones who see omens in the sky, who gaze at the stars, who make monthly predictions— let them rescue you from the disaster that is about to overtake you!
net.ISA_47_14: Look, they are like straw, that the fire burns up; they cannot rescue themselves from the heat of the flames. There are no coals to warm them, no firelight to enjoy.
net.ISA_47_15: They will disappoint you, those you have so faithfully dealt with since your youth. Each strays off in his own direction, leaving no one to rescue you.”
net.ISA_48_1: Listen to this, O family of Jacob, you who are called by the name ‘Israel,’ and are descended from Judah, who take oaths in the name of the Lord, and invoke the God of Israel— but not in an honest and just manner.
net.ISA_48_2: Indeed, they live in the holy city; they trust in the God of Israel, whose name is the Lord of Heaven’s Armies.
net.ISA_48_3: “I announced events beforehand, I issued the decrees and made the predictions; suddenly I acted and they came to pass.
net.ISA_48_4: I did this because I know how stubborn you are. Your neck muscles are like iron and your forehead like bronze.
net.ISA_48_5: I announced them to you beforehand; before they happened, I predicted them for you, so you could never say, ‘My image did these things, my idol, my cast image, decreed them.’
net.ISA_48_6: You have heard; now look at all the evidence! Will you not admit that what I say is true? From this point on I am announcing to you new events that are previously unrevealed and you do not know about.
net.ISA_48_7: Now they come into being, not in the past; before today you did not hear about them, so you could not say, ‘Yes, I know about them.’
net.ISA_48_8: You did not hear, you do not know, you were not told beforehand. For I know that you are very deceitful; you were labeled a rebel from birth.
net.ISA_48_9: For the sake of my reputation I hold back my anger; for the sake of my prestige I restrain myself from destroying you.
net.ISA_48_10: Look, I have refined you, but not as silver; I have purified you in the furnace of misery.
net.ISA_48_11: For my sake alone I will act, for how can I allow my name to be defiled? I will not share my glory with anyone else!
net.ISA_48_12: Listen to me, O Jacob, Israel, whom I summoned. I am the one; I am present at the very beginning and at the very end.
net.ISA_48_13: Yes, my hand founded the earth; my right hand spread out the sky. I summon them; they stand together.
net.ISA_48_14: All of you, gather together and listen! Who among them announced these things? The Lord’s ally will carry out his desire against Babylon; he will exert his power against the Babylonians.
net.ISA_48_15: I, I have spoken— yes, I have summoned him; I lead him and he will succeed.
net.ISA_48_16: Approach me—listen to this! From the very first I have not spoken in secret; when it happens, I am there.” So now, the Sovereign Lord has sent me, accompanied by his Spirit.
net.ISA_48_17: This is what the Lord, your Protector, says, the Holy One of Israel: “I am the Lord your God, who teaches you how to succeed, who leads you in the way you should go.
net.ISA_48_18: If only you had obeyed my commandments, prosperity would have flowed to you like a river, deliverance would have come to you like the waves of the sea.
net.ISA_48_19: Your descendants would have been as numerous as sand, and your children like its granules. Their name would not have been cut off and eliminated from my presence.
net.ISA_48_20: Leave Babylon! Flee from the Babylonians! Announce it with a shout of joy! Make this known— proclaim it throughout the earth! Say, ‘The Lord protects his servant Jacob.
net.ISA_48_21: They do not thirst as he leads them through dry regions; he makes water flow out of a rock for them; he splits open a rock and water flows out.’
net.ISA_48_22: There will be no prosperity for the wicked,” says the Lord.
net.ISA_49_1: Listen to me, you coastlands! Pay attention, you people who live far away! The Lord summoned me from birth; he commissioned me when my mother brought me into the world.
net.ISA_49_2: He made my mouth like a sharp sword, he hid me in the hollow of his hand; he made me like a sharpened arrow, he hid me in his quiver.
net.ISA_49_3: He said to me, “You are my servant, Israel, through whom I will reveal my splendor.”
net.ISA_49_4: But I thought, “I have worked in vain; I have expended my energy for absolutely nothing.” But the Lord will vindicate me; my God will reward me.
net.ISA_49_5: So now the Lord says, the one who formed me from birth to be his servant— he did this to restore Jacob to himself, so that Israel might be gathered to him; and I will be honored in the Lord’s sight, for my God is my source of strength —
net.ISA_49_6: he says, “Is it too insignificant a task for you to be my servant, to reestablish the tribes of Jacob, and restore the remnant of Israel? I will make you a light to the nations, so you can bring my deliverance to the remote regions of the earth.”
net.ISA_49_7: This is what the Lord, the Protector of Israel, their Holy One, says to the one who is despised and rejected by nations, a servant of rulers: “Kings will see and rise in respect, princes will bow down, because of the faithful Lord, the Holy One of Israel who has chosen you.”
net.ISA_49_8: This is what the Lord says: “At the time I decide to show my favor, I will respond to you; in the day of deliverance I will help you; I will protect you and make you a covenant mediator for people, to rebuild the land and to reassign the desolate property.
net.ISA_49_9: You will say to the prisoners, ‘Come out,’ and to those who are in dark dungeons, ‘Emerge.’ They will graze beside the roads; on all the slopes they will find pasture.
net.ISA_49_10: They will not be hungry or thirsty; the sun’s oppressive heat will not beat down on them, for one who has compassion on them will guide them; he will lead them to springs of water.
net.ISA_49_11: I will make all my mountains into a road; I will construct my roadways.”
net.ISA_49_12: Look, they come from far away! Look, some come from the north and west, and others from the land of Sinim.
net.ISA_49_13: Shout for joy, O sky! Rejoice, O earth! Let the mountains give a joyful shout! For the Lord consoles his people and shows compassion to the oppressed. The Lord Remembers Zion
net.ISA_49_14: “Zion said, ‘The Lord has abandoned me, the Lord has forgotten me.’
net.ISA_49_15: Can a woman forget her baby who nurses at her breast? Can she withhold compassion from the child she has borne? Even if mothers were to forget, I could never forget you!
net.ISA_49_16: Look, I have inscribed your name on my palms; your walls are constantly before me.
net.ISA_49_17: Your children hurry back, while those who destroyed and devastated you depart.
net.ISA_49_18: Look all around you! All of them gather to you. As surely as I live,” says the Lord, “you will certainly wear all of them like jewelry; you will put them on as if you were a bride.
net.ISA_49_19: Yes, your land lies in ruins; it is desolate and devastated. But now you will be too small to hold your residents, and those who devoured you will be far away.
net.ISA_49_20: Yet the children born during your time of bereavement will say within your hearing, ‘This place is too cramped for us, make room for us so we can live here.’
net.ISA_49_21: Then you will think to yourself, ‘Who bore these children for me? I was bereaved and barren, dismissed and divorced. Who raised these children? Look, I was left all alone; where did these children come from?’”
net.ISA_49_22: This is what the Sovereign Lord says: “Look I will raise my hand to the nations; I will raise my signal flag to the peoples. They will bring your sons in their arms and carry your daughters on their shoulders.
net.ISA_49_23: Kings will be your children’s guardians; their princesses will nurse your children. With their faces to the ground they will bow down to you, and they will lick the dirt on your feet. Then you will recognize that I am the Lord; those who wait patiently for me are not put to shame.
net.ISA_49_24: Can spoils be taken from a warrior, or captives be rescued from a conqueror?
net.ISA_49_25: Indeed,” says the Lord, “captives will be taken from a warrior; spoils will be rescued from a conqueror. I will oppose your adversary and I will rescue your children.
net.ISA_49_26: I will make your oppressors eat their own flesh; they will get drunk on their own blood, as if it were wine. Then all humankind will recognize that I am the Lord, your Deliverer, your Protector, the Powerful One of Jacob.”
net.ISA_50_1: This is what the Lord says: “Where is your mother’s divorce certificate by which I divorced her? Or to which of my creditors did I sell you? Look, you were sold because of your sins; because of your rebellious acts I divorced your mother.
net.ISA_50_2: Why does no one challenge me when I come? Why does no one respond when I call? Is my hand too weak to deliver you? Do I lack the power to rescue you? Look, with a mere shout I can dry up the sea; I can turn streams into a desert, so the fish rot away and die from lack of water.
net.ISA_50_3: I can clothe the sky in darkness; I can cover it with sackcloth.” The Servant Perseveres
net.ISA_50_4: The Sovereign Lord has given me the capacity to be his spokesman, so that I know how to help the weary. He wakes me up every morning; he makes me alert so I can listen attentively as disciples do.
net.ISA_50_5: The Sovereign Lord has spoken to me clearly; I have not rebelled, I have not turned back.
net.ISA_50_6: I offered my back to those who attacked, my jaws to those who tore out my beard; I did not hide my face from insults and spitting.
net.ISA_50_7: But the Sovereign Lord helps me, so I am not humiliated. For that reason I am steadfastly resolved; I know I will not be put to shame.
net.ISA_50_8: The one who vindicates me is close by. Who dares to argue with me? Let us confront each other! Who is my accuser? Let him challenge me!
net.ISA_50_9: Look, the Sovereign Lord helps me. Who dares to condemn me? Look, all of them will wear out like clothes; a moth will eat away at them.
net.ISA_50_10: Who among you fears the Lord? Who obeys his servant? Whoever walks in deep darkness, without light, should trust in the name of the Lord and rely on his God.
net.ISA_50_11: Look, all of you who start a fire and who equip yourselves with flaming arrows, walk in the light of the fire you started and among the flaming arrows you ignited! This is what you will receive from me: you will lie down in a place of pain.
net.ISA_51_1: “Listen to me, you who pursue godliness, who seek the Lord. Look at the rock from which you were chiseled, at the quarry from which you were dug.
net.ISA_51_2: Look at Abraham, your father, and Sarah, who gave you birth. When I summoned him, he was a lone individual, but I blessed him and gave him numerous descendants.
net.ISA_51_3: Certainly the Lord will console Zion; he will console all her ruins. He will make her wilderness like Eden, her arid rift valley like the garden of the Lord. Happiness and joy will be restored to her, thanksgiving and the sound of music.
net.ISA_51_4: Pay attention to me, my people. Listen to me, my people! For I will issue a decree, I will make my justice a light to the nations.
net.ISA_51_5: I am ready to vindicate, I am ready to deliver, I will establish justice among the nations. The coastlands wait patiently for me; they wait in anticipation for the revelation of my power.
net.ISA_51_6: Look up at the sky. Look at the earth below. For the sky will dissipate like smoke, and the earth will wear out like clothes; its residents will die like gnats. But the deliverance I give is permanent; the vindication I provide will not disappear.
